title: PushUrgency/QuotaScope Unknown variants missing loss-of-value doc disclaimer
description: ChatMemberRole, ChatKind, DeliveryState, OwnerPresence, and ChannelPermissionTargetType all document their Unknown variant with: 'If serialized, produces the literal string \"unknown\" — not the original wire value.' The two new enums PushUrgency and QuotaScope lack this disclaimer. ChatStreamDataType::Unknown says only 'Unrecognized value — silently ignored per spec' which is ambiguous about what happens on re-serialization. A user who stores a decoded PushUrgency::Unknown and later re-serializes it will silently corrupt the wire value with no warning. Inconsistency in doc patterns also means the existing test test_unknown_catch_all_variants_serialize_as_literal_unknown does not cover the new enums. Maintenance consequence: user confusion, divergent doc style, missing test coverage.
